title: Gonolobus pedunculatus
taxonomy: Gonolobus pedunculatus belongs to the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The genus Gonolobus is part of the subfamily Asclepiadoideae, which is a group of plants containing milkweeds and related species. This classification places Gonolobus pedunculatus within a larger group of flowering plants that share similar characteristics and growth habits. The species is native to Central and South America, where it grows in a variety of environments. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Gonolobus pedunculatus has adapted to its surroundings through unique structural and chemical features.

side_effects: Gonolobus pedunculatus, also known as the tropical milkweed vine, is a plant that may cause side effects due to its toxicity. intro: Gonolobus pedunculatus is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the 'Gonolobus vine'. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which can help to treat conditions such as fever, respiratory infections, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Gonolobus pedunculatus include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Gonolobus pedunculatus is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in ointments and salves to treat skin conditions and wounds.
